pi_items.source: Task configuration consistent with OpenLCA standard export paths for LCA results
TRACI 2.1 impact category documentation from EPA. TRACI 2.1 uses 'Global Warming' as the official impact category name for climate-related environmental impacts.
TRACI 2.1 documentation. GWP (Global Warming Potential) is standard nomenclature in LCIA for reporting climate impact in CO2-equivalents. Confirmed in EPD Guide and EPA TRACI 2.1 resources.
TRACI 2.1 official documentation. GWP results are reported in kg CO2-equivalents (kg CO2-eq or kg CO2e). The keyword 'kg CO2' will appear as part of 'kg CO2-eq' in CSV export.

Task description specifies 'Post-consumer HDPE packaging' as the waste flow name. HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene) is a commonly modeled material in LCA waste-end-of-life studies, confirmed by numerous peer-reviewed LCA studies on HDPE plastic waste treatment.

pi_items.verified_value: USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database from Federal LCA Commons
pi_items.source: USLCI is a real, publicly available database managed by NREL through the Federal LCA Commons. It is the standard US Life Cycle Inventory database containing gate-to-gate, cradle-to-gate, and cradle-to-grave data for US-based processes, including waste treatment processes.

TRACI 2.1 is a real LCIA method developed by US EPA (version 2.1 released 2012). It is widely available as part of LCIA method bundles for OpenLCA, distributed through Federal LCA Commons and OpenLCA Nexus.

pi_items.metadata_value: TRACI 2.1 for Global Warming Potential calculation
pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 (Tool for Reduction and Assessment of Chemical and Other Environmental Impacts, version 2.1)
pi_items.source: TRACI 2.1 is an official US EPA impact assessment method. EPA documentation confirms it includes Global Warming as an impact category with GWP reported in kg CO2-equivalents. Method is peer-reviewed and widely used in US LCA studies.

pi_items.verified_value: Waste flow is the correct OpenLCA flow type for modeling end-of-life waste materials. Mass in kg is standard unit.
pi_items.source: OpenLCA manual documents waste flow creation and modeling methodology. USLCI database includes landfill and waste treatment processes that accept waste flows as inputs.

pi_items.metadata_value: Landfill/waste treatment process available in USLCI database
pi_items.verified_value: USLCI contains US-specific landfill disposal processes for various waste types
pi_items.source: USLCI database documentation confirms it includes waste treatment processes. OpenLCA community documentation discusses available landfill and waste treatment processes in USLCI for modeling end-of-life scenarios.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real data and real methodology. USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) is a publicly available EPA-managed database from the Federal LCA Commons containing authentic US-based life cycle inventory data. TRACI 2.1 is a verified EPA impact assessment method (version 2.1, released 2012) that includes Global Warming Potential as a core impact category, with results reported in kg CO2-equivalents. HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene) is a standard material in LCA waste-end-of-life studies. The task requires modeling a waste flow using real USLCI landfill treatment processes and calculating genuine LCIA results using established EPA methodology. All required keywords ('Global Warming', 'GWP', 'kg CO2') are authentic terminology that will appear in TRACI 2.1 impact assessment results exported to CSV format.
